title: Angular Pipe Options Schema
description: Creates a new, generic pipe definition in the given project.

name.description: Creates a new pipe in your project. Pipes are used to transform data for display in templates. They take input values and apply a specific transformation, such as formatting dates, currency, or filtering arrays. This schematic generates the necessary files and boilerplate code for a new pipe.
The name for the new pipe. This will be used to create the pipe's class and spec files (e.g., `my-pipe.pipe.ts` and `my-pipe.pipe.spec.ts`).
name.x-prompt: What name would you like to use for the pipe?

path.description: The path at which to create the pipe, relative to the workspace root.
The path where the pipe files should be created, relative to the workspace root. If not provided, the pipe will be created in the current directory.

project.description: The name of the project where the pipe should be created. If not specified, the CLI will determine the project from the current directory.

flat.description: When true (the default) creates files at the top level of the project.

skipTests.description: Do not create "spec.ts" test files for the new pipe.
Creates the new pipe files at the top level of the current project. If set to false, a new folder with the pipe's name will be created to contain the files.
Prevent the generation of a unit test file `spec.ts` for the new pipe.

skipImport.description: Do not import this pipe into the owning NgModule.

standalone.description: Whether the generated pipe is standalone.
Do not automatically import the new pipe into its closest NgModule.

Generate a standalone pipe. Standalone pipes are self-contained and don't need to be declared in an NgModule. They can be used independently or imported directly into other standalone components, directives, or pipes.

module.description: Specify the NgModule where the pipe should be declared. If not provided, the CLI will attempt to find the closest NgModule in the pipe's path.

export.description: The declaring NgModule exports this pipe.
Automatically export the pipe from the specified NgModule, making it accessible to other modules in the application.
